title: Hmmwhats this
wikitext:
[]: Hmm...what's this??             Quote Post by cd603 » Tue Dec 06, 2011 7:00 am It doesn't really reveal anything, but I know I haven't seen it in any of the movies thus far. Very interesting...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Dec 06, 2011 7:15 am Said to be scenes cut from the movie. I have another one:

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Tue Dec 06, 2011 7:51 am What the fuck.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 06, 2011 8:15 am This looks more like something I would expect of one of the video games.

[]: One thing will lead to another... B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Tue Dec 06, 2011 8:25 am I was thinking this must be one of those weird ads, but if it's really from the movie, I guess that means they're also one-upping the "alternate reality" scenario. No wait, let me guess, Maya does Kendo and Misato is a Noh dancer. @Mr. Tines: If you're reading this, would you say this is appropriate behavior for your waifu? At least you now know she can work a kind of shaft.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Dec 06, 2011 8:34 am Hum. nearest thing to Ritsky fanservice ive seen :P Where was that strip obtained from? Gonna need some sort of context here.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Dec 06, 2011 9:50 am Jayfive wrote: Hum. nearest thing to Ritsky fanservice ive seen :P

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Dec 06, 2011 9:51 am It's either some really old fanart or one of the Eva games from the 90s. *Digs around through HD* I can't find any source for the image so it's a fanart. I've seen that before (with no film strip). Somebody had too much time on their hands and film-stripped it, it seems. Hell if I know why.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Tue Dec 06, 2011 10:08 am svenge wrote: Did you really forget Ritsuko's introduction in Episode 1?

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Tue Dec 06, 2011 11:41 am No, it's possible that the strip probably came from any of the themed pachinko machines with video monitors.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Tue Dec 06, 2011 11:48 am aaaand, the mystery is revealed. very funny, nevertheless.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Dec 06, 2011 12:21 pm http://blog.livedoor.jp/news23vip/archives/2703798.html It's a nice attempt at a photoshop, unless the edge of the film strip is not supposed to match where the picture meets the black.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Dec 06, 2011 3:38 pm I'm having flashbacks to that awful Evangelion porno...

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Tue Dec 06, 2011 6:50 pm Looks to fit the Akira Island theme, yo.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Tue Dec 06, 2011 7:11 pm Whatever it is it's photoshopped.

[]:             Quote Post by SaVaGe » Tue Dec 06, 2011 10:12 pm What. The hell.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Dec 07, 2011 3:51 am The film margin looks photoshopped. But whether the pictures themselves are just fan-art I am not so sure....

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 07, 2011 4:02 am Whoever made this image is a successful troll.

[]:             Quote Post by cd603 » Wed Dec 07, 2011 4:40 am like I said in the original post, nothing is really happening with this, but thank you for clearing it up a bit.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Dec 07, 2011 1:11 pm The character model seems to look more like the ones drawn for the original NGE TV series than it does the re-drawn models created for NME. (Either that, or similar in appearance to one of the off-model drawing styles in one of the out-sourced episodes of NGE.) I doubt any form of this image has anything to do with "Q" or "FINAL".
